name: Precision Agriculture Agent
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure IoT Hub
services.purpose: Ingest telemetry from soil moisture sensors, weather stations, drone imagery feeds, and irrigation controllers across farm zones

services.name: Azure AI Vision
services.purpose: Satellite and drone imagery analysis — crop health classification, pest/disease detection, weed identification, growth stage assessment

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for agronomic recommendations — irrigation scheduling, fertilization plans, pest management, yield forecasting, and natural language farm reports

services.name: Azure Digital Twins
services.purpose: Digital twin models of farm zones — spatial layout, soil profiles, crop rotation history, irrigation network topology, microclimate modeling

services.name: Azure Machine Learning
services.purpose: Custom ML models for yield prediction, soil nutrient forecasting, pest outbreak probability, optimal planting windows

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Agriculture API — sensor data processing, imagery analysis orchestration, recommendation engine, farm dashboard backend

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Time-series sensor data, crop health history, recommendation logs, farm zone metadata, yield records

prod.note: Dedicated throughput, analytical store for trends

enterprise.note: Multi-region, change feed for real-time alerts

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: IoT device certificates, API keys, satellite provider credentials, farm data encryption keys

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Sensor health, imagery pipeline latency, recommendation accuracy, IoT message throughput, model drift detection

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for routine sensor alert summarization and status reports, reserve GPT-4o for complex multi-modal agronomic analysis combining imagery + soil + weather — saves 65% on AI costs
Process satellite imagery in batch during off-peak hours — scheduled Functions cost 40% less than real-time processing and imagery updates are typically daily
Cache soil moisture and weather forecast data with 30-minute TTL — sensor readings change slowly and repeated queries within windows return identical results
Use IoT Hub message routing to filter low-priority telemetry at ingestion — only store and process readings that exceed change thresholds to reduce downstream costs by 50%
Train custom Vision models on farm-specific crop varieties — smaller specialized models achieve higher accuracy at lower inference cost than general-purpose models
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
